channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 35 Pakistani Universities Rank in QS World University Rankings 2026
item.description: The QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026 has featured 35 universities from Pakistan, highlighting the country’s continued academic growth on the global stage. Among these, 31 are public-sector...

item.title: Light Rain in Karachi Brings Cooler Weather and Relief from Heat
item.description: Karachi experienced light rainfall on Thursday morning, providing much-needed relief from the ongoing warm weather in the city. Drizzle was recorded in central areas such as Saddar, Clifton, MA Jinnah...

item.title: Iran and US Stalemate Persists Despite Pakistan’s Mediation Efforts
item.description: Tensions between Iran and the United States remain unresolved despite a series of indirect diplomatic efforts mediated by Pakistan. The latest developments show that while Washington has described bac...

item.title: Tanzania Leads Regional Efforts to Expand Digital Connectivity
item.description: Tanzania is leading the way in expanding digital connectivity in Africa, as 23 English-speaking countries gathered in Dar es Salaam for a high-level telecommunications workshop. The three-day ITU Subr...

item.title: How Africa’s Insurgent Groups Are Shifting from Attacks to Territorial Control
item.description: Even though terrorism is declining globally, Africa is increasingly becoming the centre of militant activity. The 2026 Global Terrorism Index shows a 28% drop in terrorism deaths and a similar fall in...

item.title: Court Verdicts Against Meta and Google Intensify Debate on Social Media Accountability
item.description: Two major jury verdicts in the United States have placed renewed scrutiny on social media giants Meta and Google. In Los Angeles, a jury ordered the two companies to pay a total of six million dollars...

item.title: Ethiopia’s banking sector faces consolidation drive amid low capital and competition
item.description: Ethiopia’s banking sector is undergoing an important transformation as regulators push for consolidation among the country’s many small lenders. For years, the financial system has been characterised ...

item.title: Numora Trading explains its limited role in the Tanzania-Burundi oil dispute arbitration
item.description: Numora Trading PTS Ltd has issued a statement clarifying its involvement in the ongoing oil dispute between Prestige Investment SA of Burundi and Lamar Commodity Trading DMCC, which is being resolved ...

item.title: Ghana Leads Drive for Global Recognition and Reparations for Slave Trade at the UN
item.description: This week marks a major moment in US–Africa relations, highlighting historical justice, commercial diplomacy, and political developments across the continent. Ghana’s President John Mahama is set to d...

item.title: How Vicoba and mobile money empower Tanzanian women and strengthen families
item.description: The article highlights how women’s collective saving through Village Community Banks (VICOBA) and the use of mobile money platforms such as M-Koba are transforming households and communities across Ta...

item.title: Tanzania Signs $300 Million Niobium Deal to Join Global Supply Chain
item.description: Tanzania has signed a $300 million deal with Panda Hill Tanzania Limited to develop the Panda Hill niobium project in Mbeya. This deal is part of a broader industrialisation strategy aimed at turning ...

item.title: Government and communities join forces to shorten school distances in Bagamoyo
item.description: In Bagamoyo District, students have long faced tough conditions walking between 14 and 30 kilometres daily to reach secondary schools. The ‘one ward, one school’ policy, once seen as a good plan, has ...

item.title: Kisumu hospital urges families to collect 47 unclaimed bodies before disposal deadline
item.description: Jaramogi Oginga Odinga Teaching and Referral Hospital (JOOTRH) in Kisumu has appealed to the public to help identify and collect 47 bodies that have remained unclaimed at its mortuary for more than th...

item.title: African countries face oil supply challenges amid Middle East disruptions
item.description: African nations are facing growing vulnerability in their oil supply due to decades of under-invested infrastructure and dwindling reserves. With the Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint for global oil,...

item.title: Jessica Sutta Suggests Political Views Led to Exclusion from Pussycat Dolls Reunion
item.description: Jessica Sutta, a former member of the Pussycat Dolls, revealed in a recent interview that she believes her conservative and Republican political stance may have played a role in her exclusion from the...

item.title: Mama Jimmy and Baba Jimmy Give a Tour of Their Newly Built US Home
item.description: Kenyan TikToker Judith, popularly known as Mama Jimmy, together with her husband Baba Jimmy, have shared a tour of their newly built house in the United States. The couple, originally from Kitale, mov...

item.title: Almost 500,000 Families to be Added to 4Ps After Verification
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) announced that up to 493,000 poor families may soon be added to the Pantawid Pamilyang Pilipino Program (4Ps) as new beneficiaries. This comes a...

item.title: Punjab Schools Set to Reopen on April 1, No Extension in Holidays
item.description: Punjab's Education Minister, Rana Sikandar Hayat, has confirmed that schools across the province will reopen on April 1, ending the current school holiday period. The announcement comes after the prov...

item.title: Petroleum Dealers Delay Strike Amid Global Fuel Crisis Due to Middle East Tensions
item.description: Petroleum dealers in Pakistan have decided to postpone a strike they had planned to begin on March 26, 2026, due to the ongoing crisis in the Middle East. The escalating conflict in the region has cau...

item.title: Petroleum Dealers Delay Strike Amid Global Fuel Shortage Concerns
item.description: Petroleum dealers in Pakistan have decided to delay a planned strike that was set to begin on March 26, 2026, due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. The Pakistan Petroleum Dealers Association (PPDA)...
